title: flat style pack (Kurzgesagt-inspired)
short_description: Ship the flat pack at parity bar — palette, typography, at least 12 geometric characters, at least 20 props, smooth-easing transition set, all golden-tested and license-vetted — and pass the founder style checkpoint on a flat-pack tech-explainer fixture (logged).
long_description_md:
# `flat` style pack (Kurzgesagt-inspired)

## Why this task exists

`flat` is the second launch style pack and the proof that anima-ai's pack boundary is real rather than a `crude`-specific renderer with configuration attached. It supplies the calm, precise tech/science pole of the product: clean geometric construction, deliberate color relationships, generous whitespace, and smooth motion. It must feel unmistakably distinct from the `crude` pack while avoiding the generic corporate-explainer middle that the design spec explicitly rejects.

This task completes **Phase 5: `flat` pack + taste constraints + continuity**. It consumes the versioned, data-only `StylePack` contract from `style-pack-system` and the pinned-container raster-regression workflow from `golden-image-harness`; it must not introduce a renderer fork, ambient randomness, runtime downloads, or executable pack hooks. The exact resolved pack version remains part of the determinism contract and must flow through rendering, verification, inspection, and artifact metadata.

The pack is inspired by the flat geometric explainer genre, not a source of copied proprietary characters, artwork, or trademarks. Bundled art must be original/procedural or independently licensed for redistribution, and every bundled font and asset must have complete, shipped license and attribution records.

## Scope and public behavior

Ship a production `flat` pack at an exact initial content version using the shared schema:

- A named role-based palette and bundled typography appropriate for high-contrast technical explainers. Scene code continues to use semantic roles rather than raw colors; no system-font lookup is allowed.
- At least 12 distinct geometric character entries and at least 20 distinct props in the real asset manifest. Every declared pose or variant must resolve to package-local resources and have complete tags, license metadata, and attribution coverage.
- A smooth-easing transition set expressed only through the stable core animation and easing registries. Defaults should favor intentional, continuous motion such as fade, crossfade, wipe, or paper-slide with smooth non-bouncy easing; they must remain renderer-neutral data rather than flat-specific compositor code.
- Golden registration and committed baselines for all flat-specific rendered elements: palette/typography fixtures, each character and declared pose, each prop and declared variant, and transition/easing fixtures. Canonical baselines are blessed only inside the pinned golden container; local comparison remains tolerance-aware.
- A representative multi-beat tech-explainer fixture that exercises flat typography, geometric characters, props, transitions, and cross-scene camera state. It is both the Phase 5 style-review artifact and a regression fixture.

The task also completes the pack-aware taste and continuity layer. Register `PACING.BEAT_STALL`, `PACING.ENTRANCE_STORM`, `PACING.SCENE_DENSITY`, `CONTINUITY.COLOR_DRIFT`, and `CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT` as stable, deterministic verification rules. Each rule must use compiled timeline/geometry state rather than rendered-pixel inspection, emit the standard actionable `Finding` shape, have a versioned registry entry, and take validated thresholds and default severity from the resolved pack's constraint profile.

`CONTINUITY.COLOR_DRIFT` must compare recurring semantic identities across scenes, including persisted map identities where applicable, by reading the persisted map-state identifiers the map-camera-state task defines (territory/camera identity records) — this task does not recreate or reinterpret map-state persistence, it only consumes the identifiers map-camera-state already exposes. Add a typed, serializable continuity identity only where existing stable node, asset, or map-state identifiers cannot express the relationship; do not infer identity from display text, color proximity, or raster pixels. `CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT` must compare resolved camera state across scene boundaries and allow an intentional declared transition while flagging an unannounced discontinuity above the profile threshold.

Thresholds must be demonstrably pack-specific: the same borderline pacing, entrance-density, scene-density, color-continuity, and camera-boundary fixtures must produce the configured flat findings while the intentionally more permissive crude profile tolerates them. Taste findings may default to warnings, but project-level configuration must be able to promote or demote each supported rule between `warning` and `error`. The report metadata must expose the resolved pack default, any project override, and the effective severity so a project cannot silently weaken verification.

Finally, prove pluggability with one fixed source fixture rendered and verified once under `crude` and once under `flat`; the only authored source change may be the exact style-pack ID/version pin. The fixture must not contain pack-name conditionals, raw colors, renderer branches, or fallback asset catalogs.

## Integration points

- `style-pack-system` owns immutable pack loading, exact version resolution, manifest validation, asset discovery, and the role-based palette contract. This task supplies validated flat data and the Phase 5 constraint-profile entries through that interface.
- `golden-image-harness` owns baseline identity, comparison, pinned-container generation, and CI failure artifacts. Flat coverage must register with that harness rather than creating a second snapshot mechanism.
- `crude-pack` supplies the parity-fixture content: the pluggability proof deliverable and the "fixture coverage in both launch packs" requirement on the five taste/continuity rules both need crude's fixtures and profile already existing to compare against, so this task depends on crude-pack, not the reverse.
- `geometry-resolver` supplies the compiled timeline/geometry state (resolved bounds, transform tracks) that the taste rules (`PACING.*`) and continuity rules (`CONTINUITY.*`) evaluate; these rules read resolved geometry, never rendered pixels or raw scene-graph nodes.
- The animation system supplies stable transition and easing IDs; flat chooses defaults but does not add backend-specific effects or duplicate core animation semantics.
- The verification report schema supplies finding ordering, rule versions, pass calculation, JSON output, and report metadata. New rules must use that existing path.
- `map-camera-state` owns persisted camera and territory identity/state. `CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT` and the map-identity portion of `CONTINUITY.COLOR_DRIFT` read that persisted state and identifiers directly; this task must not recreate or duplicate map-state persistence.

## Explicitly out of scope

- Changes to the shared renderer architecture, timeline compiler, map datasets, or core easing/transition semantics.
- A third style pack, a marketplace, remote asset fetching, automatic pack upgrades, AI-generated assets, VLM review, or user-authored executable pack code.
- Replacing the Phase 7/launch long-form acceptance bar. This task establishes a focused flat tech-explainer fixture and founder checkpoint, not the final 15–20 minute episode.

deliverables.description: Ship the exact-versioned `flat` StylePack with its role-based palette, bundled typography, validated smooth transition defaults, at least 12 geometric characters, and at least 20 props. Validate real package resources, manifest IDs, poses/variants, deterministic inventory ordering, complete per-asset license metadata, and shipped attribution records; do not use copied proprietary artwork or unvetted fonts/assets.

Register golden coverage for every flat-specific rendered pack element: palette and typography fixtures, every character pose, every prop variant, and the flat transition/easing set. Baselines must be addressable through the shared golden registry, generated only in the pinned environment, and compared here compare-only against the baselines already committed and blessed in the pinned golden container — this deliverable never generates baselines itself (generation is a pinned-container, human-blessed step owned by golden-image-harness; a compare-only check cannot rubber-stamp itself).

Implement the five Phase 5 taste and continuity rules with validated per-pack profiles and fixture coverage in both launch packs: `PACING.BEAT_STALL`, `PACING.ENTRANCE_STORM`, `PACING.SCENE_DENSITY`, `CONTINUITY.COLOR_DRIFT`, and `CONTINUITY.CAMERA_TELEPORT`. Add visible per-project warning/error severity overrides, and prove that a single source fixture verifies and renders under both `crude` and `flat` with only the exact pack pin changed.

Pass and log the founder style checkpoint using the flat tech-explainer fixture. The checkpoint must judge the contact sheet and low-resolution preview for calm geometric precision, generous whitespace, coherent palette/typography, and smooth motion—not merely render success—and record the decision, artifact metadata, fixture revision, and any resulting rule or asset follow-up in `docs/cofounder/LEARNINGS.md`.

verification.steps: Run `anima verify --project tests/fixtures/flat_tech_explainer/project.py --json` and confirm it passes. Generate an annotated contact sheet and low-resolution preview with `anima preview --project tests/fixtures/flat_tech_explainer/project.py`. The founder reviews those artifacts, records pass/fail plus concrete observations and metadata in `docs/cofounder/LEARNINGS.md`, and any failed judgment becomes a tracked asset or constraint-profile follow-up before re-review.
